description: Clean kickoff meeting with clear scope decisions, NFRs, and explicit feature areas. Tests baseline story extraction, epic mapping to existing epics, and NFR identification.

candidate_stories.title: Support PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and plain text document processing
candidate_stories.description: Implement document processing pipeline supporting five file formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and plain text. This is a core v1 requirement for the text extraction pipeline feeding into risk assessment.
acceptance_criteria: PDF files are parsed and text content extracted
DOCX, PPTX, and XLSX files are parsed using appropriate libraries
Plain text files are accepted without conversion
Unsupported file types return a clear error message
priority_signals: Alex: 'The file extraction pipeline is a big chunk of work on its own'
Listed as one of five core v1 feature areas
candidate_stories.source_citation: Alex: 'I'd add document processing to that list explicitly — we need to support PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and plain text. The file extraction pipeline is a big chunk of work on its own.'

candidate_stories.title: Implement five user roles with configurable permissions
candidate_stories.description: Implement role-based access control with five roles: Administrator, Master Analyst, Analyst, Export Control, and User. Permissions are configurable through a JSON file.
acceptance_criteria: Five roles are defined: Administrator, Master Analyst, Analyst, Export Control, User
Each role has distinct permissions configurable via JSON
Password hashing uses bcrypt
File-based sessions with express-session are implemented
priority_signals: Listed as one of five core v1 feature areas
Explicit role list decided in meeting
candidate_stories.source_citation: Alex: 'For auth, we'll use bcrypt for password hashing, file-based sessions with express-session, and we're planning five user roles — Administrator, Master Analyst, Analyst, Export Control, and a basic User role. Each role gets different permissions configurable through a JSON file.'

candidate_stories.title: Implement review queue audit trail from day one
candidate_stories.description: Log every review queue action (submit, claim, approve, decline, release) with the user identity and timestamp. This is a compliance requirement that should not be deferred.
acceptance_criteria: Every review action is logged with action type, user, and timestamp
Audit log is queryable by action type and user
Audit trail cannot be modified or deleted by non-admin users
Audit entries include: submit, claim, approve, decline, release
priority_signals: Alex: 'That's a compliance requirement we shouldn't defer'
Sarah confirmed: 'The review audit trail is in scope for v1'
candidate_stories.source_citation: Alex: 'we should plan for the review queue to have an audit trail from day one. Every action — submit, claim, approve, decline, release — gets logged with who did it and when. That's a compliance requirement we shouldn't defer.'

candidate_stories.title: Integrate Anthropic Claude and OpenAI as LLM providers
candidate_stories.description: Support Anthropic Claude and OpenAI as LLM providers for v1, with Gemini and Azure OpenAI as stretch goals.
acceptance_criteria: Claude is available as an LLM provider option
OpenAI is available as an LLM provider option
Users can switch between providers in settings
Both providers produce consistent output format for risk assessment
candidate_stories.source_citation: Sarah: 'Decision 6: LLM provider support in v1 will include Anthropic Claude and OpenAI. Gemini and Azure OpenAI are stretch goals.'

candidate_stories.title: Handle image-only PDFs gracefully without OCR
candidate_stories.description: When an image-only PDF is uploaded and no text can be extracted, return a meaningful error rather than proceeding with empty input. OCR is out of scope for v1.
acceptance_criteria: Image-only PDFs are detected when text extraction returns empty
A clear error message is returned to the user
The system does not attempt LLM evaluation on empty content
OCR is not included in v1 scope
priority_signals: Mike identified this as an explicit scope boundary
candidate_stories.source_citation: Mike: 'One more: OCR for image-only PDFs. We'll handle the case gracefully — if there's no extractable text, we return a meaningful error — but we're not integrating an OCR library in v1.'

candidate_stories.title: Add loading indicator for LLM evaluation calls
candidate_stories.description: Add a frontend loading indicator during LLM evaluation calls so users know the system is working, since evaluations take 5-30 seconds.
acceptance_criteria: Loading spinner or progress indicator appears when evaluation starts
Indicator remains visible until evaluation result is returned
User is informed that AI evaluation may take 5-30 seconds
priority_signals: Mike suggested it in context of response time discussion
candidate_stories.source_citation: Mike: 'And we should add a loading indicator on the frontend for evaluation calls so users know the system is working.'

candidate_stories.title: Target 30 concurrent users for initial release
candidate_stories.description: The initial release must support 30 concurrent users with the current single-server SQLite architecture. The 500 concurrent user target is deferred as a future NFR requiring architecture changes.
acceptance_criteria: System performs acceptably under 30 concurrent user sessions
Load test validates 30 concurrent users without degradation
Architectural constraints for the 30-user ceiling are documented
Scaling plan for 500 users documented as future work
candidate_stories.source_citation: Sarah: 'For now, let's document it this way: target 500 concurrent users as a future NFR, but the initial release targets 30 concurrent users with the current architecture. We'll revisit scaling when usage data tells us we need to.'

candidate_stories.title: API response time under 2 seconds for non-LLM endpoints
candidate_stories.description: All non-LLM API endpoints (review queue, filtering, user management) must respond within 2 seconds. This is a hard requirement from stakeholders.
acceptance_criteria: All non-LLM API endpoints respond within 2 seconds under normal load
Response time monitoring is in place for key endpoints
Endpoints exceeding 2 seconds are flagged for optimization
LLM evaluation endpoints are excluded from this requirement
priority_signals: Sarah: 'Let's make that a hard requirement'
candidate_stories.source_citation: Sarah: 'Let's make that a hard requirement: API response time under 2 seconds for all non-LLM endpoints.'
